segments.title: HMS Orion's wide-ranging wartime service
segments.summary: HMS Orion's battle honours trace a remarkably broad Royal Navy war, from the Atlantic in 1939 to Mediterranean and north-west European operations in 1944.
segments.body: Dr Clarke runs through the engagements attached to Orion's record, beginning with Atlantic service in 1939 and continuing through Calabria, Mediterranean operations, Matapan, Greece and Crete. The later list includes Sicily, Salerno, the Aegean, Anzio, Normandy and the south of France. It presents the Leander-class cruiser as a ship repeatedly committed across the major theatres open to the Royal Navy.

evidence.note: The transcript lists Orion's Atlantic, Mediterranean, Greek, Italian, Aegean, Normandy and southern-France battle honours.

segments.title: Why battle-honour totals need context
segments.summary: A comparison with HMS Nubian, HMS Jervis and Warspite explains why battle-honour totals are not a simple ranking of a ship's wartime contribution.
segments.body: Orion is compared with HMS Nubian and HMS Jervis, while Warspite is said to sit ahead in the official count. Dr Clarke cautions that the categories reflect the type of ship as well as its service: a battleship's presence and gunfire could qualify as a battle, while cruisers and destroyers accumulated campaign honours under different expectations. The distinction makes Orion's long record more meaningful than a raw comparison of totals and supports the video's verdict that she was a war hero.

evidence.note: The transcript compares Orion with Nubian, Jervis and Warspite, then explains the different battle and campaign-honour treatment of battleships, cruisers and destroyers.
